mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns are negative in 60% of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and drive negative equity returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in over 55% of months where UNRATE rises above the 0.4pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based economic recovery, boosting corporate earnings forecasts and producing positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a win rate above 52% in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in the industrial production index during liquidity crises trigger margin calls and risk-off flows, causing immediate negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO growth does not correspond to negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in 65% of observed stress periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls indicate strengthening economic activity and higher wage growth, which supports consumer demand and generates positive monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: Positive PAYEMS revisions yield negative 20-day forward returns in more than 50% of monthly observations over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Core inflation exceeding 3.0% forces central banks to accelerate rate hikes, increasing discount rates and compressing equity valuations over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 60-day returns in over 60% of quarters where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 0.3% monthly threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising housing starts and falling retail sales signals a sector rotation away from consumer discretionary, dragging down broad market returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The HOUST-RSAFS divergence signal produces a positive 20-day return win rate above 54% across all tested market regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production rebounds above its 6-month moving average during deflationary periods, confirming supply chain normalization and triggering rapid short-term equity rallies.
failure_condition: INDPRO crossovers above the 6-month moving average fail to generate positive 5-day returns in 70% of deflationary recovery episodes.
